persona.name: Frank Novak

occupation.title: Owner / Lead Technician
occupation.description: Runs a 3-bay auto body shop with two employees. Does estimates, manages insurance claims, and still gets under the hood himself. On his feet 10 hours a day on concrete floors. Built the business from nothing 20 years ago.

persona.education: High school diploma from Broken Arrow High School. Vocational certification in automotive technology from Tulsa Tech. Learned business management by doing it.
long_term_goals: Keep the shop profitable through the EV transition
Retire by 62 and spend winters fishing in Texas
Not end up on his wife's 'health project list'
See his daughter graduate nursing school
Replace the shop roof before it leaks again
persona.style: Frank says what he means in as few words as possible. He's blunt, funny in a dry way, and deeply suspicious of anything that sounds like marketing. He evaluates products the way he evaluates car parts: does it work, how long does it last, what does it cost. He has no interest in trends, influencers, or lifestyle branding. He wears steel-toed boots to work, jeans and a polo on weekends, and whatever shoes his wife bought him at Walmart. He'll engage thoughtfully if you speak plainly, but he'll dismiss you in a sentence if you don't.

traits: Blunt and direct — no patience for embellishment
Skeptical of anything marketed to people unlike him
Self-made and proud of it — trusts his own experience
Generous with opinions, stingy with compliments
Low-maintenance in every aspect of personal life
Secretly enjoys being asked for his opinion
Funny in a deadpan, self-aware way

interests: Classic car restoration (1969 Camaro project in the back bay)
Morning walks with the dog (recent, wife's idea)
likes: Products that last without requiring maintenance
Shoes he can buy without going to a specialty store
Brands he recognizes from TV or the store shelf
dislikes: Being in a store where staff are younger than his daughter
Products that need an app or account to use
The concept of paying $150 for shoes you wear outside
Being told he needs something he doesn't think he needs
Running — he considers it voluntary suffering

beliefs: Shoes are shoes — they protect your feet and shouldn't cost more than a car payment
If a product needs a marketing campaign to sell, it probably isn't that good
Exercise is what you get from working — he doesn't need to pay for it
The more adjectives a product has, the less it's worth
skills: Auto body repair, paint, and frame straightening
Small business management and insurance negotiation
Fishing — knows every spot on Grand Lake
Evaluating products by durability and value

general: Walks the dog for 15 minutes every morning — his only 'exercise'
Buys the same shoe model until it's discontinued, then complains
Asks his wife or daughter for product recommendations
Won't enter a specialty store voluntarily

morning: Walk the dog around the block
evening: Watch the game or a show
Maybe tinker with the Camaro in the garage
weekend: Sunday: church, then Sooners game or family visit

health.physical: Overweight (5'11", 220 lbs) but not alarmed by it. Knees ache after long days on concrete — chalks it up to age. Lower back stiffness from decades of bending over cars. Doctor mentioned cholesterol; Frank said he'd think about it. Decent arm and grip strength from manual labor.
health.mental: Stable and content. Not introspective. Occasional stress about the business during slow months. Sleeps well. Would never describe himself as having mental health needs.

relationships.name: Debbie Novak
relationships.description: Dental office manager. The organized one in the marriage. Bought him walking shoes last Christmas. Quietly managing his health from the sidelines.

relationships.name: Ashley Novak
relationships.description: Nursing student at OU. Tries to get Frank to take health seriously. He listens to her more than he admits.

relationships.name: Tommy Novak
relationships.description: Works at a car dealership in OKC. They bond over OU football and fishing trips.

relationships.name: Hank the Dog
relationships.description: Frank's morning walking companion. The only reason he exercises.
